entries.summary: Phase 7 complete: test fixture (sample.docx) created, docx-intake.test.js with 44 tests across 7 categories covering mammoth extraction, autoRun flag simulation, file extension validation, path validation, error handling, adversarial/edge cases, and fixture validation. Multiple reviewer iterations to remove tautological tests, fix misleading test names, add missing adversarial coverage. Final test count: 224/224 (167 sanitizer + 13 smoke + 44 docx-intake).
top_rejection_reasons: Tautological assertions testing JS variable assignments instead of app logic
Test count claims not matching actual count
Misleading test names not matching actual behavior
lessons_learned: loadDocxFile cannot be unit-tested directly because it is not exported from app.js and requires DOM APIs — simulation tests are the pragmatic approach but must avoid tautological assertions (testing JS semantics instead of app logic)
Test count claims in comments should be avoided — let the test framework report the count to prevent mismatches
Misleading test names (e.g., 'handles corrupt file' that actually tests 'missing file') are caught by adversarial review — always match test name to actual behavior
Path traversal security tests should test BOTH the extension check AND the isAbsolute guard — testing only one layer gives a false sense of coverage
Type confusion tests (typeof guards for non-string inputs) are cheap to add and catch a common Electron IPC vulnerability class
